description:
You are playing the role of a log-analyst who has been asked to audit a collection of legacy INI configuration
    files for unusually high threshold settings.

    1.  INPUT DATA LOCATION  
        • Three INI files are already present in  
          /home/user/data/configs  
          and are the only files inside that directory.  
        • Do not move, delete or modify those files.

    2.  WHAT YOU MUST CREATE (exact names and paths)  
        a) /home/user/analysis/threshold_audit.log  
           ‑ plain-text, one finding per line, **no header line**.  
           ‑ Only list keys whose numeric value is strictly greater than 75.  
           ‑ Format for every line (no extra spaces):  
                <filename>:<section>:<key>=<value>  
             Example (do **NOT** include this exact line):  
                foobar.ini:thresholds:cpu=92  
           ‑ Preserve original key order as they appear in each file.  
           ‑ List files alphabetically (app1.ini, app2.ini, …).  
           ‑ A final newline character is required at the end of the file.

        b) /home/user/analysis/threshold_stats.csv  
           ‑ Comma-separated values, **UTF-8**, with a single header row followed by one data row per INI file.  
           ‑ Header **must** be exactly:  
                filename,total_threshold_keys,keys_over_75,average_threshold  
           ‑ The 4 columns are:  
                1. filename  – just the basename (e.g. app1.ini)  
                2. total_threshold_keys – count of keys that appear inside the [thresholds] section  
                3. keys_over_75 – how many of those keys have a value > 75  
                4. average_threshold – arithmetic mean of all numeric values inside [thresholds],  
                   rounded to two decimal places (use 2 digits even if .00)  
           ‑ Rows must be sorted by filename in ascending order.  
           ‑ A final newline character is required at the end of the file.

    3.  ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (automated tests will verify)  
        • The two output files exist at precisely the paths stated above.  
        • Their contents match the formats and values described.  
        • No other files are created or modified inside /home/user, except the new /home/user/analysis directory and its two files.

    4.  HINTS  
        • Standard CLI tooling such as awk, grep, sed, cut, tr, sort, paste, bc, python, etc. is available.  
        • Be mindful of trailing/leading spaces – the graders are strict.  
        • Confirm your work by displaying both files to terminal once you think you’re done.

    Produce the two artefacts exactly as described to finish the task.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing files that the agent will parse.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/data/configs/app1.ini"><![CDATA[
[general]
name = Alpha
version = 1.2.0

[network]
port = 8080
host = 127.0.0.1

[thresholds]
cpu = 85
memory = 65
disk = 90
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/configs/app2.ini"><![CDATA[
[general]
name = Beta
version = 2.4.1

[network]
port = 9090
host = 192.168.1.10

[thresholds]
cpu = 50
memory = 75
disk = 60
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/configs/app3.ini"><![CDATA[
[general]
name = Gamma
version = 3.1.5

[network]
port = 7070
host = 10.0.0.5

[thresholds]
cpu = 95
memory = 80
disk = 70
temperature = 88
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
    Expected artefacts after successful task completion.
    -->
    <expected_output>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/threshold_audit.log"><![CDATA[
app1.ini:thresholds:cpu=85
app1.ini:thresholds:disk=90
app3.ini:thresholds:cpu=95
app3.ini:thresholds:memory=80
app3.ini:thresholds:temperature=88
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/analysis/threshold_stats.csv"><![CDATA[
filename,total_threshold_keys,keys_over_75,average_threshold
app1.ini,3,2,80.00
app2.ini,3,0,61.67
app3.ini,4,3,83.25
]]></file>
    </expected_output>
